batch_metadata.note: Oct 3 RF-04 165-row cluster present. This cluster does NOT meet RF-07 hot-period criteria (triage hot periods are Aug 2024, May 2025, Aug 2025; Oct 2024 is not a hot period). Flooding threshold assessment: cluster density requires raw timestamp confirmation not available from RF-04 batch data.

arc_state_snapshot.note: ARC-RF07-001 ACTIVE. No new signals this batch. Oct 3 RF-04 cluster (165-row range): note that this cluster is in Phase A, outside the triage hot periods, and without individual timestamps in the RF-04 batch record. Per ADD-001, flooding requires confirmation of 5+ messages in a 10-minute window. Given that (1) Oct 2024 is not an RF-07 hot period, (2) the cluster lacks timestamp granularity in available data, and (3) the Oct behavioral shift (Melissa going largely silent after mid-October, INF-005) suggests she is winding down rather than escalating, this cluster is held without a primary flag. Synthesis-level timestamp review may revisit.

batch_observations.tone_assessment: Mixed — Sep 2024 moderate conflict; Oct 3 large RF-04 cluster; then behavioral inflection point (INF-005) as Melissa reduces communication volume after mid-October conflict.
batch_observations.rf07_assessment: Zero RF-07 primary signals. Oct 3 cluster is the batch's most notable event but falls outside the RF-07 hot-period framework and lacks timestamp granularity for flooding confirmation. Post-hot-period (Aug 2024 anchor captured in B-017). Arc active but no new signals. The Oct 2024 behavioral silence (INF-005) begins at the end of this batch — this silence will shape B-020's low message count.
batch_observations.custodial_context: Sep-Oct 2024 parenting-time windows present. Oct behavioral shift means contact drops significantly by batch end — the inverse of custodial-time-overstepping.
